notes: Marathwada (incl. Aurangabad) hit by chronic drought since 2012; 2014-15 rainfall 44% below normal region-wide
Beed among 5 worst-hit Marathwada districts; SPI -1.68 (extreme drought) 2014-15; worst-affected again in 2018
Latur SPI -1.56 (extreme drought) 2014-15; city faced acute water crisis (2016 water-train episode) in Marathwada drought
Osmanabad (Dharashiv) SPI -1.75, the most extreme drought value among Marathwada districts in 2014-15
Jalna one of 8 Marathwada districts reeling under drought since 2013; part of the 5 worst-affected group
Parbhani among 5 worst-hit Marathwada districts; groundwater depletion of -2.87m linked to drought years
Hingoli among 5 worst-hit Marathwada districts; highest regional groundwater depletion (-4.35m)
Nanded among 5 worst-hit Marathwada districts reeling under drought since 2013
Kalaburagi in North Interior Karnataka; declared drought-hit for Rabi 2015-16, deficient rainfall since 2012-13
Vijayapura among North Interior Karnataka districts declared drought-affected 2015-16; Rs 10 crore relief released
Bidar declared drought-affected for Rabi 2015-16 among 12 North Interior Karnataka districts
Barmer among 13 districts hit by 2016-17 famine; 2018-19 saw 131 villages severely drought-declared; 61 famine events in 100 yrs
Jaisalmer worst-hit in 2018-19 declaration: 632 villages severely drought-affected; chronic desert drought recurrence
Jodhpur among 4 districts with villages declared drought-affected Nov 2019 (297 moderate, 13 severe)
Kutch part of Saurashtra-Kutch region hit hard in the 1987 famine (17 of 19 Gujarat districts affected) and recurring dry cycles
Rural Banaskantha still recalls the 1986-87 'dukaal' hard-time drought; drought officially declared in Banaskantha in 2012-13
Chhatarpur among Bundelkhand districts declared drought-affected in MP's 2015-16 declaration (46/50 districts) and acute 2019 drought
Sagar named among worst Bundelkhand-MP drought districts in the 2015-16 declaration and 2019 acute drought (36 districts)
Mahasamund named among Chhattisgarh districts affected by drought in November 2000 alongside Raipur, Rajnandgaon and Durg
Chitrakoot is one of the 13 Bundelkhand districts; region saw 24-56% rainfall deficits across 2004-08 and drought again in 2015-16
Banda is a core Bundelkhand district; region-wide rainfall deficits of 24-56% recorded 2004-08, drought recurred 2015-16
Gaya explicitly named among Bihar districts declared drought-hit in 2009 (ReliefWeb); also a poor-monsoon district in 2010 and 2013
Palamu worst-affected by drought in 2008; severe drought 2006; Palamu division among hardest-hit again in 2022 Jharkhand drought
